member.name: Jim Shannon
member.synopsis: Jim Shannon is the Democratic Unionist Party MP for Strangford , and has been an MP continually since 6 May 2010.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Central African Republic: Religion
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what discussions she has had with the Government of the Central African Republic and the United Nations Multidimensional Integrated Stabilization Mission in the Central Afric
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK is deeply concerned by the violence that took place in Haut-Mbomou Prefecture on 29 June and the reports of civilian casualties. We remain in regular contact with the Government of the Central African Republic (CAR) and the United Nations Multidime...

writtenQuestions.heading: Cybercrime
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what assessment has been made of trends in the level of cybercrime affecting people and small businesses.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Cybercrime causes huge damage to people and businesses across the UKand is a priority for the Government. We keep cybercrime trends under close review. Survey evidence suggests that the prevalence of cybercrime affecting individuals and small businesses h...

writtenQuestions.heading: Hate Crime
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what steps he is taking to improve support and protection for victims of hate crimes, including monitoring and reporting mechanisms.
writtenQuestions.answerText: There is robust legislative framework in place to respond to hate crimes which target race, religion, sexual orientation, disability, and transgender identity.The Government funds an online hate crime reporting portal, True Vision, designed so victims of ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Crime: Internet
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what steps are being taken to improve the effectiveness of crime reporting apps and online reporting systems.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Since 2016, the Home Office has invested over £170m in improving and modernising contact channels between the public and local police forces. Online crime reporting is available for all forces across England and Wales either via local force websites or th...

writtenQuestions.heading: Mental Health Services: Prisoners
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps are being taken to improve mental health support for prisoners.
writtenQuestions.answerText: NHS England commissions prison health care services into every prison in England to the equivalence of the community. NHS England’s Service Specification for integrated mental health services in prisons states that patients within secure settings should r...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Aviation: Carbon Emissions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of aviation emissions on the environment.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises that aviation has a range of environmental impacts, including greenhouse gas emissions and non-CO₂ impacts which could have warming effects on the climate; and local noise and air quality impacts. The Government is committed to d...

writtenQuestions.heading: Ports: Freight
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what steps she is taking to improve the efficiency of freight transport and reduce bottlenecks at ports.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The multimodal UK freight and logistics sector delivers reliable, resilient and efficient movement of goods to support UK business and the consumer. The government recognised the importance of the sector in our Industrial Strategy (2025). We have recently...

writtenQuestions.heading: Railways: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what recent assessment she has made of the adequacy of rail punctuality and reliability.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Passenger rail performance improved in Great Britain in the first quarter of this year (January to March 2026) compared to the first quarter of last year (January to March 2025), with a 'Time to 3' average of 86.4 per cent (recorded station stops arrived ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Railways: Overcrowding
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what steps she is taking to reduce overcrowding on commuter rail services.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department expects train operators to plan and operate appropriate capacity in response to expected demand. Department for Transport Operator works with train operating companies to consider measures to address crowding, for example strengthening serv...

writtenQuestions.heading: Railway Network: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what funding she has allocated to maintain and modernise rail infrastructure.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The recent Spending Review confirmed £10.2billion for rail infrastructure enhancements over the next four years. Network Rail’s funding for the operation, maintenance and renewal of the network from 2024 to 2029 was set at £44.1 billion, excluding enhance...

writtenQuestions.heading: Aviation: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what steps she is taking to help reduce flight delays at major airports.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK aviation market operates predominantly in the private sector. UK airports and airlines are therefore responsible for ensuring appropriate contingency plans are in place to minimise potential disruption. The Department regularly engages with the UK ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Aviation: Compensation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what recent steps have been taken to improve passenger compensation and rights for delayed or cancelled flights.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Air passenger rights remain a priority for the Government. UK passengers continue to have strong rights under Regulation (EC) No. 261/2004 in the event of delays, cancellations and denied boarding, including rights to compensation in some circumstances. I...

writtenQuestions.heading: Electric Vehicles
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what steps she is taking to support the uptake of electric and zero-emission vehicles.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is investing £7.5 billion to support industry and drivers to switch to zero emission vehicles, including the £2 billion Electric Car Grant which has helped over 135,000 drivers choose a sustainably manufactured zero emission car. A further ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Air Traffic Control
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment she has made of the capacity of UK air traffic control systems to handle passenger and cargo volumes safely.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Air Traffic Control services in the UK are operated by NATS En Route Ltd, under a licence issued by the Secretary of State and regulated by the Civil Aviation Authority (CAA).NERL is required to assess and make decisions about how it best manages UK airsp...

writtenQuestions.heading: Airports: Noise
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what steps she is taking to help reduce noise pollution from airports.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government sets noise controls at Heathrow, Gatwick and Stansted airports. These controls reflect the need to balance the impact from airports on communities with the benefits that they bring to the UK economy. At other airports, appropriate noise con...

writtenQuestions.heading: Domestic Abuse
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what steps she is taking to reduce domestic abuse and support victims.
writtenQuestions.answerText: On 18th December 2025, this government published the ‘Freedom from Violence and Abuse; a cross-cutting strategy’, setting out our whole-system approach to halve violence against women and girls in a decade. The Strategy is backed by at least £1 billion of...

writtenQuestions.heading: Organised Crime
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of organised crime groups on local communities.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The NCA is charged with undertaking an annual assessment of the serious and organised crime (SOC) threat to the UK, including analysis of emerging and evolving trends in this threat. The National Strategic Assessment (NSA) 2026 of Serious Organised Crime ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Counter-terrorism: Information Sharing
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what steps she is taking to help improve counter-terrorism intelligence sharing between agencies.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Counter-terrorism is a whole-of-government effort in response to a terrorist threat that is diverse, dynamic and complex. This work is underpinned by the UK’s counter-terrorism strategy, CONTEST.Collaboration and information sharing is supported via regul...

writtenQuestions.heading: Shipping: Carbon Emissions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of shipping emissions on air quality in coastal communities.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises that emissions from shipping can affect air quality in coastal areas, particularly near ports and busy shipping routes, and is taking action through the International Maritime Organization (IMO) to reduce emissions from ships. Th...

writtenQuestions.heading: Emergencies: Planning
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, what steps he has taken to ensure continuity of government operations during extreme events such as flooding and cyberattacks.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As part of prudent resilience planning the COBR Directorate has a number of operational arrangements in place for the continuity of crisis management against a range of national security incidents. These plans include response to severe weather and infras...

items.text: Name of donor: THE FORB FOUNDATION, Matthew Jones, Secretariat to APPG FoRB Address of donor: 5 Thornbury Close, Merthyr Tydfil, CF48 1HP Estimate of the probable value (or amount of any donation): Accommodation including some meals (£320), flights (£305.53) and other food (£222.21), value £847.74 Destination of visit: Iraq (Erbil) Dates of visit: 15 February 2026 to 20 February 2026 Purpose of visit: Part of a delegation to Iraq to look at the state of FoRB in Kurdistan, from the perspective of churches that are registered there. We met the Prime minister and Minister of Foreign affairs who is responsible for FoRB. We also looked at the economy and how faiths are looked after as refugees within Kurdistan. (Registered 5 March 2026)
